title: TKNN Invariant As First Chern Class Of The Filled-Band Bundle
summary: For a gapped two-dimensional band insulator, the TKNN invariant is the first Chern number of the filled-band bundle over the Brillouin torus, equivalently written through the occupied-projector formula and the Berry curvature of the occupied states.
assumptions: The two-dimensional band structure is fully gapped over the whole Brillouin zone.
The occupied-state subspaces vary smoothly with momentum.
scope: Definition of the single-particle topological invariant used in Witten Lecture Two and in TKNN.

source_anchors.notes: Witten defines k' as the first Chern class of the occupied-state bundle over the Brillouin torus.
Primary source for the geometric Hall-conductance invariant written through occupied-bundle curvature.

representation: First Chern number of the occupied-state bundle over the two-dimensional Brillouin torus.
motivation: This note exists because Lecture Two needs a microscopic integer invariant that matches the macroscopic quantized Hall response. The TKNN invariant is that band-theoretic integer: it packages the topology of the filled-band bundle into a number that later gets identified with the Hall level.
prerequisite_story: Before reading this note, know the Berry connection and Berry curvature of the filled-state bundle and understand that a smooth occupied subspace over the Brillouin torus defines a vector bundle. In the rank-one case the invariant is an ordinary U(1) Chern number; for several filled bands one uses the traced curvature or the occupied-projector formula.
local_context_story: In Witten Lecture Two, this definition appears after the Chern-Simons description of the Hall response and before the argument that the many-body Hall coefficient equals the band invariant. It is the microscopic topological quantity denoted by k' in the lecture.
forward_links_story: This definition feeds the projector-formula proof fragments and then the theorem equating the integer Hall response with both the band and many-body Chern numbers. It is also the natural two-dimensional analogue of the Lecture One Berry-flux integral around a node.
canonical_questions: Why is the TKNN invariant a first Chern number of the occupied-state bundle rather than an ad hoc integral?
How do the curvature formula and the projector formula define the same integer?
What changes when there is more than one filled band?
Why is this definition not yet the full theorem that measured Hall conductivity equals the invariant in the many-body setting?
scope_boundaries: This is a band-topology definition of the invariant itself, not yet the theorem that the interacting many-body Hall response equals the same integer.
The simple rank-one formula uses an abelian connection; for several filled bands the invariant is written with Tr F or with the occupied-projector commutator formula.

math_expressions.notes: Single-particle Hall invariant defined from the Berry curvature of the filled bands.
Equivalent projector formula for the same first Chern number.
Rank-one specialization when a single filled band is present.

symbols.meaning: occupied-state subbundle of the full Bloch Hilbert bundle
Berry curvature of the occupied-state bundle

failure_modes: If the bulk gap closes anywhere on the Brillouin torus, the occupied-state bundle and its Chern number cease to be well-defined.
faithfulness_strategy: Keep the canonical definition equal to Witten's k' and the TKNN occupied-bundle Chern number, while exposing the projector formula explicitly for the multi-band case.
faithfulness_notes: The KB adds explanatory wording about rank-one versus multi-band presentations, but the invariant itself remains the same first Chern number.
comparator_risks: Do not weaken the definition into a rank-one-only statement when the source also supports the traced-curvature and projector formulations.
attribution_requirements: Retain attribution to both Witten Lecture Two and the original TKNN paper when citing this canonical definition.
Do not present the projector formula as KB-internal invention; it is a source-derived equivalent expression.
admissible_assumptions: Work with a smooth gapped occupied-state bundle over the two-dimensional Brillouin torus.
Treat the multi-band case through the occupied projector or traced curvature rather than through one global abelian gauge.
formalization_blockers: A reusable Lean-side interface for smooth Bloch bundles over the Brillouin torus is still missing.
The passage from local Berry-connection charts to a global first-Chern-class representative is not yet packaged as a lower-level geometry lemma.
retrieval_hints: Use when a query asks for the precise microscopic invariant behind the integer Hall response.
